-- $Id: testes/events.lua $
-- See Copyright Notice in file all.lua

print('testing metatables')

local debug = require'debug'

X = 20; B = 30

_ENV = setmetatable({}, {__index=_G})

collectgarbage()

X = X+10
assert(X == 30 and _G.X == 20)
B = false
assert(B == false)
_ENV["B"] = undef
assert(B == 30)

assert(getmetatable{} == nil)
assert(getmetatable(4) == nil)
assert(getmetatable(nil) == nil)
a={name = "NAME"}; setmetatable(a, {__metatable = "xuxu",
                    __tostring=function(x) return x.name end})
assert(getmetatable(a) == "xuxu")
assert(tostring(a) == "NAME")
-- cannot change a protected metatable
assert(pcall(setmetatable, a, {}) == false)
a.name = "gororoba"
assert(tostring(a) == "gororoba")

local a, t = {10,20,30; x="10", y="20"}, {}
assert(setmetatable(a,t) == a)
assert(getmetatable(a) == t)
assert(setmetatable(a,nil) == a)
assert(getmetatable(a) == nil)
assert(setmetatable(a,t) == a)


function f (t, i, e)
  assert(not e)
  local p = rawget(t, "parent")
  return (p and p[i]+3), "dummy return"
end

t.__index = f

a.parent = {z=25, x=12, [4] = 24}
assert(a[1] == 10 and a.z == 28 and a[4] == 27 and a.x == "10")

collectgarbage()

a = setmetatable({}, t)
function f(t, i, v) rawset(t, i, v-3) end
setmetatable(t, t)   -- causes a bug in 5.1 !
t.__newindex = f
a[1] = 30; a.x = "101"; a[5] = 200
assert(a[1] == 27 and a.x == 98 and a[5] == 197)

do    -- bug in Lua 5.3.2
  local mt = {}
  mt.__newindex = mt
  local t = setmetatable({}, mt)
  t[1] = 10     -- will segfault on some machines
  assert(mt[1] == 10)
end


local c = {}
a = setmetatable({}, t)
t.__newindex = c
t.__index = c
a[1] = 10; a[2] = 20; a[3] = 90;
for i = 4, 20 do a[i] = i * 10 end
assert(a[1] == 10 and a[2] == 20 and a[3] == 90)
for i = 4, 20 do assert(a[i] == i * 10) end
assert(next(a) == nil)


do
  local a;
  a = setmetatable({}, {__index = setmetatable({},
                     {__index = setmetatable({},
                     {__index = function (_,n) return a[n-3]+4, "lixo" end})})})
  a[0] = 20
  for i=0,10 do
    assert(a[i*3] == 20 + i*4)
  end
end


do  -- newindex
  local foi
  local a = {}
  for i=1,10 do a[i] = 0; a['a'..i] = 0; end
  setmetatable(a, {__newindex = function (t,k,v) foi=true; rawset(t,k,v) end})
  foi = false; a[1]=0; assert(not foi)
  foi = false; a['a1']=0; assert(not foi)
  foi = false; a['a11']=0; assert(foi)

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# run_official_test.sh <test_file>
#
# Wraps a single Lua 5.4 official test file (reference/lua-c/testes/*.lua)
# with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
# launcher set up (e.g. _soft, _port, _nomsg, arg) and feeds the whole
# thing into the lua-rs CLI. Captures stdout+stderr, prints a one-line
# PASS/FAIL summary, and (on failure) the first 5 lines of failure
# context.
#
# Usage:
#   ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/vararg.lua

set -uo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"

TEST_TIMEOUT_S=${TEST_TIMEOUT_S:-30}
BIN="$ROOT/target/debug/lua-rs"
OUT_DIR="harness/impl/official"
mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"

if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
    echo "usage: $0 <test_file>" >&2
    exit 2
fi

TEST_FILE="$1"
if [ ! -f "$TEST_FILE" ]; then
    echo "[err] test file not found: $TEST_FILE" >&2
    exit 2
fi

if [ ! -x "$BIN" ]; then
    echo "[err] lua-rs binary not built at $BIN" >&2
    exit 2
fi

BASE="$(basename "$TEST_FILE" .lua)"
COMBINED="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.combined.lua"
OUTFILE="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.out"

PREAMBLE='-- harness preamble: emulate the globals lua-c testes/all.lua sets
_soft = true
_port = true
_nomsg = true
_U = false
arg = arg or {}
_G = _G or _ENV
if _VERSION == nil then _VERSION = "Lua 5.4" end
'

{ printf '%s\n' "$PREAMBLE"; cat "$TEST_FILE"; } > "$COMBINED"

TESTES_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$TEST_FILE")" && pwd)"
export LUA_PATH="$TESTES_DIR/?.lua;$TESTES_DIR/?/init.lua;./?.lua;./?/init.lua"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# run_official_test.sh <test_file>
#
# Wraps a single Lua 5.4 official test file (reference/lua-c/testes/*.lua)
# with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
# launcher set up (e.g. _soft, _port, _nomsg, arg) and feeds the whole
# thing into the lua-rs CLI. Captures stdout+stderr, prints a one-line
# PASS/FAIL summary, and (on failure) the first 5 lines of failure
# context.
#
# Usage:
#   ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/vararg.lua

set -uo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"

TEST_TIMEOUT_S=${TEST_TIMEOUT_S:-30}
BIN="$ROOT/target/debug/lua-rs"
OUT_DIR="harness/impl/official"
mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"

if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
    echo "usage: $0 <test_file>" >&2
    exit 2
fi

TEST_FILE="$1"
if [ ! -f "$TEST_FILE" ]; then
    echo "[err] test file not found: $TEST_FILE" >&2
    exit 2
fi

if [ ! -x "$BIN" ]; then
    echo "[err] lua-rs binary not built at $BIN" >&2
    exit 2
fi

BASE="$(basename "$TEST_FILE" .lua)"
COMBINED="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.combined.lua"
OUTFILE="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.out"

PREAMBLE='-- harness preamble: emulate the globals lua-c testes/all.lua sets
_soft = true
_port = true
_nomsg = true
_U = false
arg = arg or {}
_G = _G or _ENV
if _VERSION == nil then _VERSION = "Lua 5.4" end
'

{ printf '%s\n' "$PREAMBLE"; cat "$TEST_FILE"; } > "$COMBINED"

TESTES_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$TEST_FILE")" && pwd)"
export LUA_PATH="$TESTES_DIR/?.lua;$TESTES_DIR/?/init.lua;./?.lua;./?/init.lua"

run_with_timeout() {
    local src_file="$1"
    local out_file="$2"
    local src
    src="$(cat "$src_file")"
    if command -v gtimeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        gtimeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    elif command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        timeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    else
        ( "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1 ) &
        local pid=$!
        ( sleep "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" && kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null ) &>/dev/null &
        local watcher=$!
        wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null
        local rc=$?
        kill "$watcher" &>/dev/null
        wait "$watcher" 2>/dev/null || true
        return $rc
    fi
}

run_with_timeout "$COMBINED" "$OUTFILE" || true

reached_exec=0
grep -qE "^\[4/4\] Executing chunk" "$OUTFILE" && reached_exec=1

if grep -qE "^\[ok\] execution completed" "$OUTFILE" \
    && ! grep -qE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
    printf "PASS  %s\n" "$TEST_FILE"
    exit 0
fi

if grep -qE "not yet implemented:" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="stub"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*parser" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="parse-panic"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*codegen|panicked at .*compil" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="codegen-panic"
elif grep -qE "^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at " "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="runtime-panic"
elif grep -qE "^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
    if [ "$reached_exec" = "1" ]; then
        cause="runtime-error"
    else
        cause="parse-or-compile-error"
    fi
else
    cause="unknown"
fi

printf "FAIL  %s  (cause=%s, reached_exec=%s)\n" "$TEST_FILE" "$cause" "$reached_exec"
echo "--- first 5 lines of failure context ---"
{
    grep -nE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]|panicked at " "$OUTFILE" \
        || tail -n 20 "$OUTFILE"
} | head -n 5
echo "--- (full output at $OUTFILE) ---"
exit 1

delta.text: I found the bug. In `debug_lib.rs:198`, `state.set_top(2)` calls the inherent stub (`
state.rs:1287`) which sets `top` to an **absolute** stack index. But the C `l
ua_settop(L, 2)` is **frame-relative**. When `debug.setmetatable(10, mt)` is called from inside `events.lua` at a deep call frame, `set
_top(2)` absolute drops the metatable arg, so `set_metatable` sees the integer `10` as the metatable and panics with

"table expected".

Other stdlib files use `lua_vm::api::set_top(state, N)?` which correctly does the
frame-relative conversion. I'll fix the debug_lib.rs misuses.

content.text:
I found the bug. In `debug_lib.rs:198`, `state.set_top(2)` calls the inherent stub (`state.rs:1287`) which sets `top` to an **absolute** stack index. But the C `lua_settop(L, 2)` is **frame-relative**. When `debug.setmetatable(10, mt)` is called from inside `events.lua` at a deep call frame, `set_top(2)` absolute drops the metatable arg, so `set_metatable` sees the integer `10` as the metatable and panics with "table expected".

Other stdlib files use `lua_vm::api::set_top(state, N)?` which correctly does the frame-relative conversion. I'll fix the debug_lib.rs misuses.

content.text:
Build is clean. No temporary instrumentation was added (only edits to swap inherent `state.set_top(N)` for the frame-relative `lua_vm::api::set_top(state, N)?`).

**Root cause:** `debug_lib.rs:198` (debug.setmetatable) called `state.set_top(2)`, which resolves to the inherent stub at `state.rs:1287` that takes an **absolute** stack index — but the original C `lua_settop(L, 2)` is **frame-relative**. When `debug.setmetatable(10, mt)` is called from within `events.lua` at a deep call frame, the absolute interpretation drops the `mt` argument; the subsequent `state.set_metatable(1)` then sees the integer `10` as the metatable value and panics with "table expected" at `api.rs:1642`.

**Fix:** Switched the misuses in `debug_lib.rs` (5 sites) and `auxlib.rs` (2 sites) to call `lua_vm::api::set_top(state, N)?`, matching the pattern already used correctly in `base.rs`, `string_lib.rs`, `math_lib.rs`, `table_lib.rs`, and `loadlib.rs`. The inherent `state.set_top` (absolute) remains for VM-internal callers in `vm.rs`/`do_.rs`/`tagmethods.rs`/`debug.rs`, which is its documented purpose.

**New state:** `cargo build -p lua-cli` is green (warnings only, no errors). I did not re-run `events.lua` per task instructions; the mega-loop will re-test.

Build is clean. No temporary instrumentation was added (only edits to swap inherent `state.set_top(N)` for the frame-relative `lua_vm::api::set_top(state, N)?`).

**Root cause:** `debug_lib.rs:198` (debug.setmetatable) called `state.set_top(2)`, which resolves to the inherent stub at `state.rs:1287` that takes an **absolute** stack index — but the original C `lua_settop(L, 2)` is **frame-relative**. When `debug.setmetatable(10, mt)` is called from within `events.lua` at a deep call frame, the absolute interpretation drops the `mt` argument; the subsequent `state.set_metatable(1)` then sees the integer `10` as the metatable value and panics with "table expected" at `api.rs:1642`.

**Fix:** Switched the misuses in `debug_lib.rs` (5 sites) and `auxlib.rs` (2 sites) to call `lua_vm::api::set_top(state, N)?`, matching the pattern already used correctly in `base.rs`, `string_lib.rs`, `math_lib.rs`, `table_lib.rs`, and `loadlib.rs`. The inherent `state.set_top` (absolute) remains for VM-internal callers in `vm.rs`/`do_.rs`/`tagmethods.rs`/`debug.rs`, which is its documented purpose.

**New state:** `cargo build -p lua-cli` is green (warnings only, no errors). I did not re-run `events.lua` per task instructions; the mega-loop will re-test.
